definitions: To make something into a cylindrical or spherical shape by rolling.
To arrive, especially in a casual or unexpected way.
To increase or accumulate over time.
To prepare and smoke a hand-rolled cigarette or joint.
examples: She decided to roll up her sleeves and get to work.
A new car rolled up to the curb.
The interest will roll up over the years.
He likes to roll up his own cigarettes.
